data.title: China International Capital Plans to Acquire Brokerage Firms Cinda Securities, Dongxing Securities
Wall Street indexes end rocky session higher; Nvidia gains after hours
data.description:
Key points:
  • Target drops after missing quarterly comparable sales estimates
  • Indexes briefly pare gains after Fed minutes
  • September US jobs report due on Thursday
  • Indexes: Dow up 0.1%, S&P 500 up 0.4%, Nasdaq up 0.6%

By Caroline Valetkevitch

U.S. stocks ended a choppy session higher on Wednesday, bouncing off recent losses as technology stocks rose ahead of Nvidia's quarterly results.

Nvidia shares gained more than 5% after ending the regular session up 2.8% as the artificial intelligence leader forecast fourth-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates. Shares of Advanced Micro Devices rose 2.8% after the bell, while Alphabet was up 1.6% and Palantir Technologies was up 4%.

"The market is breathing a big, collective sigh of relief because Nvidia is confirming that AI demand is strong," said Adam Sarhan, chief executive of 50 Park Investments in New York.

"If it sticks and stays by tomorrow's close ... this little pullback in the market could end." The S&P 500 is still down more than 3% from its October highs.

Indexes briefly pared gains during the regular session after minutes from the last Federal Reserve meeting showed policymakers cautioned that lower borrowing costs could undermine the fight against inflation. The Fed cut rates by a quarter of a percentage point at each of its meetings in September and October.

Worries about a cooling labor market persisted ahead of Thursday's release of the September U.S. jobs report following the record-long U.S. government shutdown. The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics said it will not publish the October employment report, but will instead combine nonfarm payrolls for that month with November's report.

Nvidia's results have been seen as a test for the AI-driven rally that has powered the market to record highs this year.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 47.03 points, or 0.10%, to 46,138.77, the S&P 500 gained 24.84 points, or 0.38%, to 6,642.16 and the Nasdaq Composite advanced 131.38 points, or 0.59%, to 22,564.23.

Investors have worried about how companies will be able to make money from their huge investments in AI.

Among decliners, Target shares fell 2.8% after the retailer reported a bigger-than-expected drop in quarterly sales as cash-strapped U.S. consumers cut back on discretionary spending.

Shares of Walmart , which is due to report earnings on Thursday before the bell, ended 0.8% lower.

Declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 1.59-to-1 ratio on the NYSE, where there were 55 new highs and 211 new lows.

On the Nasdaq, 1,846 stocks rose and 2,851 fell, as declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 1.54-to-1 ratio.

The S&P 500 posted 12 new 52-week highs and 22 new lows while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 48 new highs and 305 new lows.

Volume on U.S. exchanges was 17.46 billion shares, compared with the roughly 20.2 billion average for the full session over the last 20 trading days.

data.title: PLATTS: 100--Mich. coal plant to continue operations through winter after latest DOE order
data.description: (Platts)--19Nov2025/529 pm EST/2229 GMT **DOE cites low winter energy reserves for extension **Extends previously existing emergency order A retirement-ready coal plant in Michigan will continue to operate after the Trump administration issued another three-month emergency order to keep the facility open. The extension was expected. In a third US Department of Energy emergency order, which was issued after the close of business on Nov. 18, US Energy Secretary Chris Wright cited North American Electric Reliability Corp. assessments indicating winter energy reserves may be low during extreme conditions. NERC issued its 2025-2026 report on Nov. 18, projecting a sharp increase of 2.5% in winter peak demand over the next several months — but it said reliability risks were limited in the MISO region. "MISO was able to procure 6.1% more resources through the annual planning reserve auction than required by its minimum resource adequacy target," NERC said. "A further 3.3 GW of resources were available but not chosen to be committed for the winter season." CMS Energy's 1,410 MW J.H. Campbell plant on the Lake Michigan shoreline had been scheduled to shut down May 31, but Wright on May 23 told the utility to call off the closure. Wright said at that time that the plant was needed to keep the Midcontinent ISO stable during the hot summer months and prevent potentially dangerous outages. In a lawsuit pending in the US Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia, Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel and clean energy groups alleged the emergency assessment was "fabricated." Several environmental groups made similar claims in a second Campbell plant lawsuit that is moving through the same court. At the time the Michigan lawsuit was filed, a DOE spokesperson cited President Donald Trump's Jan. 20 executive order declaring a national energy emergency as a justification for the retirement delay. CMS Energy said in October that keeping the Campbell plant running cost the company $80 million during the first two emergency periods. The utility expects to file for a cost recovery with the US Federal Energy Regulatory Commission by the end of 2025, with MISO ratepayers picking up the tab. — Karin Rives, karin.rives@spglobal.com-- Edited by Richard Rubin, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: Awaysis Capital, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Awaysis Capital, Inc., a company specializing in the management and development of resort properties, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities, reflecting its ongoing transition from a shell company to an active player in the hospitality and real estate sectors.

Financial Highlights

Revenue: $95,198

The increase in revenue from the three months ended September 30, 2024, to 2025 was a result of increased booking and rental revenues, management fee income related to the completion of two buildings at Casamora, and the acquisition of Chial Mountain Limited.

Operating Income: $(639,468)

Operating losses were primarily driven by operating expenses incurred as the company continued to scale its hospitality operations under the Awaysis brand and advanced preparations for a registered offering of its securities.

Net Loss: $(651,548)

The decrease in net loss from the three months ended September 30, 2024, to 2025 was principally driven by increased revenue generated from the addition of Chial Mountain Limited and related rental and management income.

Basic and diluted net loss per common share: $(0.00)

This reflects the company's ongoing transition from being a shell company into an operating business.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments:

The company generates revenue primarily from short-term unit rentals, management services, and real estate sales. These activities are focused on residential vacation home communities in desirable travel destinations.

Geographical Performance:

The company's operations are primarily based in Belize, where it manages and develops resort properties. The focus is on transforming resort properties into residential-resort enclaves, capitalizing on global trends towards remote work and extended stays.

Sales Units:

The company is involved in the sale of developed resort inventory, including condominiums and villas, as part of its broader development and growth strategy.

Future Outlook:

The company plans to expand its inventory of units and marketing footprint over time, with a focus on compliance with legal and regulatory requirements in all jurisdictions of operation. It aims to enhance its operational infrastructure and invest in branding and marketing initiatives to support growth.

SEC Filing: Awaysis Capital, Inc. [ AWCA ] - 10-Q - Nov. 19, 2025

data.title: American Strategic Investment Co. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
American Strategic Investment Co., a company primarily focused on managing office properties in Manhattan, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational challenges amid a difficult market environment influenced by the COVID-19 pandemic.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue from tenants: $36.8 million, reflecting a decrease compared to the previous year due to lower rental rates and occupancy challenges.
  • Operating loss before gain on disposition of real estate investments: $(39.6) million, indicating continued operational challenges prior to asset sales.
  • Net income (loss) and Net income (loss) attributable to common stockholders: $(14.5) million, showing improvement from the previous year's loss due to gains on asset sales.
  • Net income (loss) per share attributable to common stockholders — Basic: $(5.70), reflecting the overall net loss for the period.
  • Net income (loss) per share attributable to common stockholders — Diluted: $(5.70), consistent with the basic earnings per share.

Business Highlights

  • Geographical Performance: The company's portfolio consists primarily of office properties located in Manhattan, which have been adversely impacted by shifts in market demand following the COVID-19 pandemic. The New York City office market continues to face challenges as leasing and occupancy trends have slowed, affecting the company's revenue streams.
  • Operational Challenges: The company has faced significant liquidity constraints due to sustained declines in rental income, constrained cash flow from operations, and ongoing debt service obligations. These challenges are compounded by the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on the commercial real estate market.
  • Property Management: The company owns six properties consisting of 0.7 million rentable square feet, excluding one property in a consensual foreclosure process. The management of these properties is conducted through the company's advisor and property manager, both under common control with AR Global Investments, LLC.
  • Future Outlook: Management has developed a plan to alleviate substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern. This includes paying related party fees in shares, selling one of the company's performing assets within the next twelve months, and the advisor's willingness to lend funds if necessary.
  • Tenant and Lease Management: The company has successfully executed lease transactions to mitigate vacancy risk, although new leases have been executed at market rental rates below prior contractual rates, resulting in decreased revenue compared to historical levels.
  • Impairment Charges: The company recorded significant impairment charges on several properties, including 1140 Avenue of the Americas, 400 E. 67th Street/200 Riverside, and 196 Orchard Street, to reduce their carrying values to estimated fair values.
  • Property Disposition: During the reporting period, the company lost its economic interest and control over the 1140 Avenue of the Americas property due to a consensual foreclosure process, resulting in the removal of related assets and liabilities from the balance sheet.
  • Lease and Revenue Recognition: The company continues to recognize revenue from lease contracts on a straight-line basis over the initial term of the lease, with a weighted-average remaining lease term of 6.2 years as of September 30, 2025.

SEC Filing: American Strategic Investment Co. [ NYC ] - 10-Q - Nov. 19, 2025

data.title: Virtus Total Return Fund Inc. Announces Distributions and Discloses Sources of Distribution — Section 19(a) Notice
data.description:
HARTFORD, Conn.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--November 19, 2025--

Virtus Total Return Fund Inc. today announced the following monthly distributions:

 
Ticker  Amount of Distribution  Ex-Date/Record Date    Payable Date 
------  ----------------------  -------------------  ----------------- 
 ZTR            $0.05            December 11, 2025   December 30, 2025 
------  ----------------------  -------------------  ----------------- 
 ZTR            $0.05            January 12, 2026    January 29, 2026 
------  ----------------------  -------------------  ----------------- 
 ZTR            $0.05            February 12, 2026   February 26, 2026 
------  ----------------------  -------------------  -----------------

The Fund previously announced the following monthly distribution on August 27, 2025:

 
Ticker  Amount of Distribution  Ex-Date/Record Date    Payable Date 
------  ----------------------  -------------------  ----------------- 
 ZTR            $0.05            November 13, 2025   November 26, 2025 
------  ----------------------  -------------------  -----------------

Under the terms of its Managed Distribution Plan, the Fund will seek to maintain a consistent distribution level that may be paid, in part or in full, from net investment income and realized capital gains, or a combination thereof. Shareholders should note, however, that if the Fund's aggregate net investment income and net realized capital gains are less than the amount of the distribution level, the difference will be distributed from the Fund's assets and will constitute a return of the shareholder's capital. You should not draw any conclusions about the Fund's investment performance from the amount of this distribution or from the terms of the Fund's Managed Distribution Plan.

The Fund provides this estimate of the sources of the distributions:

                     Per Share    of Current    Per Share        of Current 
     (Sources)         Amount    Distribution     Amount        Distribution

Information regarding the Fund's performance and distribution rates is set forth below. Please note that all performance figures are based on the Fund's net asset value (NAV) and not the market price of the Fund's shares. Performance figures are not meant to represent individual shareholder performance.

 
October 31, 2025 
----------------------------------------------------------------------  ------

----------------------------------------------------------------------  ------

----------------------------------------------------------------------  ------

----------------------------------------------------------------------  ------ 
Fiscal YTD Cumulative Distribution Rate (5)                              7.78% 
----------------------------------------------------------------------  ------ 
 (2)  Average Annual Total Return on NAV is the annual compound return 
       for the five-year period. It reflects the change in the Fund's 
       NAV and reinvestment of all distributions. 
 (3)  Annualized Current Distribution Rate is the current distribution 
       rate annualized as a percentage of the Fund's NAV at month 
       end. 
 (4)  Fiscal YTD Cumulative Total Return on NAV is the percentage 
       change in the Fund's NAV from the first day of the fiscal year 
       to this month end, including distributions paid and assuming 
       reinvestment of those distributions. 
 (5)  Fiscal YTD Cumulative Distribution Rate is the dollar value of 
       distributions from the first day of the fiscal year to this 
       month end as a percentage of the Fund's NAV at month end.

The amounts and sources of distributions reported in this notice are estimates only and are not being provided for tax reporting purposes. The actual amounts and sources of the distributions for tax purposes will depend on the Fund's investment experience during the remainder of its fiscal year and may be subject to changes based on tax regulations. The Fund or your broker will send you a Form 1099-DIV for the calendar year that will tell you what distributions to report for federal income tax purposes.

About the Fund

Virtus Total Return Fund Inc. is a diversified closed-end fund whose investment objective is capital appreciation, with income as a secondary objective. Virtus Investment Advisers, LLC, a registered investment adviser affiliated with Virtus Investment Partners, Inc., is the investment adviser and Duff & Phelps Investment Management Co. and Newfleet Asset Management are the subadvisers to the Fund.

For more information on the Fund, contact shareholder services at (866) 270-7788, by email at closedendfunds@virtus.com, or through the Closed-End Funds section of virtus.com.

Fund Risks

An investment in a fund is subject to risk, including the risk of possible loss of principal. A fund's shares may be worth less upon their sale than what an investor paid for them. Shares of closed-end funds may trade at a premium or discount to their NAV. For more information about the Fund's investment objective and risks, please see the Fund's annual report. A copy of the Fund's most recent annual report can be accessed through the Closed-End Funds section of virtus.com and may be obtained free of charge by contacting "Shareholder Services" as set forth at the bottom of this press release.

About Duff & Phelps Investment Management Co.

Duff & Phelps Investment Management Co. pursues investment strategies with exceptional depth of resources and expertise. With more than 35 years of experience managing investment portfolios, Duff & Phelps has earned a reputation as a leader in investing in global listed infrastructure, global listed real estate, clean energy, and diversified real assets in institutional separate accounts and open- and closed-end funds. For more information, visit dpimc.com.

About Newfleet Asset Management

Newfleet Asset Management provides comprehensive fixed income portfolio management in multiple strategies. The Newfleet Multi-Sector Strategies team that manages the Virtus Total Return Fund Inc. employs active sector rotation and disciplined risk management in portfolio construction, avoiding interest rate bets, and remaining duration neutral to each strategy's stated benchmark. Newfleet Asset Management is a division of Virtus Fixed Income Advisers, LLC, which is a registered investment adviser affiliated with Virtus Investment Partners. For more information, visit newfleet.com.

About Virtus Investment Partners, Inc.

Virtus Investment Partners is a distinctive partnership of boutique investment managers singularly committed to the long-term success of individual and institutional investors. We provide investment products and services from our investment managers, each with a distinct investment style and autonomous investment process, as well as select subadvisers. Investment solutions are available across multiple disciplines and product types to meet a wide array of investor needs. Additional information about our firm, investment partners, and strategies is available at virtus.com.

data.title: PLATTS: 904--Platts Renewable Penetration Indices - 19Nov25
CFO Viggiano Surrenders 1,514 Of Twilio Inc [TWLO]
data.description:
Viggiano Aidan Chief Financial Officer of Twilio Inc [TWLO] filed a Form 4 on November 19, 2025 with the Securities and Exchange Commission disclosing the following changes in ownership:

Date11/17/25
TransactionSurrender(1)
Shares1,514
Price$123.28
Value$186,642

Ending Holdings: 128,104 - Direct

  • (1)
  • Surrender of shares back to company to pay option exercise cost and/or resulting tax liability.
